comics.title: Europa Missions
comics.alt: Before resurfacing, they promise to inspect the ice for any evidence of hockey-playing life.

comics.title: Make It Myself
comics.alt: It's not as big a loss as it looks, because now I have leftover supplies, which will help me talk myself into doing this all over again with a new project!

comics.title: Countdown Standard
comics.alt: Anyone who is caught counting 'three ... two ... one ... zero ... GO!' will be punished with a lifetime of eating only ISO standard food samples.

comics.title: Lightning
comics.alt: Maybe you should wear one too? I guess I'm taller than you, so as long as I have one we're fine.

comics.title: Overton
comics.alt: I think I accidentally installed an Overton window in my bedroom. A few months ago, the sun wasn't in my face in the morning, but now it is.

comics.title: Grammar
comics.alt: Communication is one of the most popular ways to transmit information, ahead of rivals such as

comics.title: Day Counter
comics.alt: It has been −2,147,483,648 days since our last integer overflow.

comics.title: Creation
comics.alt: This xkcd.com update introduces a variety of new reading modes which can be activated through the menu below the comic.

comics.title: Home Solar
comics.alt: "While I try to do my part to destroy the environment, I try not to focus too much on individual responsibility. By pushing for broad policy changes, we can collectively do far more damage to the biosphere than any of us could on our own."

comics.title: Satellite Pollution
comics.alt: We're working to make sure the images are as up-to-date and accurate as possible, with a minimum number of sponsored galaxies.

comics.title: Centimeter Wavelengths
comics.alt: Yes, the cosmic microwave background is great, but what about the earthly microwave foreground?

comics.title: Inflation Timeline
comics.alt: Depending what corners of the internet you hang out on, 'regular' may at times awkwardly coexist with 'sexy.'

comics.title: Star Formation
comics.alt: It's ok, I still have some nice, cool gas clouds that aren't collapsing. As long as nothing ionizes them, I can continue to enjoy their ... HEY! NO!!!

comics.title: Landscape Features
comics.alt: 'Well, there's speculation that it's due to a mantle hotspot.' --a geologist who's trying to cover up the fact that they didn't hear your question

comics.title: Rotational Gravity
comics.alt: I don't get it. The peak acceleration for passengers was WAY lower than in the giant-waterslide-loop-the-loop incident the other cruise line fired me for.

comics.title: Planets and Bright Stars
comics.alt: An old astronomer trick for distinguishing the Sun from other stars is to take multiple photos a few minutes apart and overlay them, making the Sun stand out due to its high proper motion.

comics.title: Subduction Retrieval
comics.alt: Aww, the oceanic crust and the continental crust are getting married!

comics.title: Home Remedies
comics.alt: As always, you are permitted to call one person for guidance, but that person must be a grandparent.

comics.title: Bazookasaurus
comics.alt: In contrast to the deep booming sound associated with the cannon in pop culture depictions, recent studies show it actually made more of a 'toot toot!' noise.

comics.title: Solar Warning
comics.alt: This replaces the previous solar activity watch, which was issued last month when the sun took off its sunglasses.
